arts.ttl: Daily Schmankerl: Real Madrid, FC Barcelona to battle for Bayern Munich’s Jamal Musiala?; Manchester City to see roster fallout if Pep Guardiola leaves?; Newcastle, Arsenal eyeing Leroy Sané; Bayern, Real, PSG among teams eyeing William Saliba; and MORE!
arts.sum: Not only are English powers like Manchester City, Arsenal FC, and Liverpool FC already dreaming about what a Musiala transfer might look like, but both Real Madrid and FC Barcelona are reportedly pondering a future with the Germany international. As Bayern Munich works to get a new deal done with Jamal Musiala, the potential competition for him — should he actually hit the transfer market next summer — could be intense.

arts.ttl: Liverpool & Real Madrid linked - Could Jamal Musiala leave Bayern Munich next season?
arts.sum: Bayern Munich are under pressure to reach a number of necessary goals this season. First and foremost, new head coach Vincent Kompany must regain control in the Bundesliga and...

arts.ttl: Passive Income: Bayern Munich set to receive share of Marvin Cuni transfer funds
arts.sum: Earlier this week, Rubin Kazan had announced that they had officially completed the signing of former Bayern Munich youth team player Marvin Cuni. From the transfer, Bayern is set to receive roughly €1 million, which is about half of the fee Kazan paid to Frosinone to secure the signing of the 23-year-old (via @iMiaSanMia_GER ).

arts.ttl: Bayern Munich’s Dayot Upamecano explains that Vincent Kompany gives him “a lot of advice” as he looks to shake off rough start to season
arts.sum: This will no doubt help the Frenchman’s self-confidence ahead of his games for Bayern, starting with Holstein Kiel this weekend, as the Bavarian giants look to collect their fourth win from four games under new head coach Vincent Kompany. Bayern Munich ’s Dayot Upamecano was heavily criticized for his start to the Bundesliga season and was duly dropped from the France starting lineup for their game against Italy.

arts.ttl: Holstein Kiel’s Fiete Arp ready to face off with Bayern Munich
arts.sum: Even before we had made promotion clear, people in the city were saying: Next year Bayern will come to Kiel! Of course, Arp is referencing Holstein Kiel’s shocking DFB-Pokal win over Bayern Munich back in 2021.

arts.ttl: Bayern Munich starlet Paul Wanner still undecided between Germany and Austria
arts.sum: Actually, I want to play in the Bundesliga for a whole year to see where it goes, but maybe the decision will be made sooner. He also rejected the chance to play for Germany U21s during the latest international break, simply because he was managing his fitness.

arts.ttl: Bayern Munich’s Joshua Kimmich left baffled by decision to exclude Jamal Musiala from Ballon D’or Top 30
arts.sum: The decision to leave Jamal Musiala off the list of the Top 30 contenders for the Ballon d’Or, the award for the best individual player in the world, was met with plenty of shock and confusion around the world, but all the more so in Germany. Germany’s players were not immune to the shock either and new Germany captain Joshua Kimmich weighed in on the topic.

arts.ttl: Bayern Munich’s Michael Olise continues to give amusingly basic interview answers as he speaks about idols and goals for the season
arts.sum: The introductory press conference of new Bayern Munich signing Michael Olise was fairly funny in how journalists struggled to get long answers out of the former Crystal Palace man, who rarely answered questions with more than one sentence. Make journalists work for the answers they want

arts.ttl: Holstein Kiel vs Bayern Munich: 2024/25 Bundesliga full coverage
arts.sum: The international break is over and Bayern Munich return to action with a game against Holstein Kiel. Vincent Kompany is currently undefeated since joining on as coach, and he’ll want to keep it that way.

arts.ttl: Holstein Kiel vs Bayern Munich: Lineups, team news, can Kompany keep finding ways to bench Palhinha, and more!
arts.sum: Bayern Munich somehow escaped the international break without suffering any new injuries, which is an inauspicious sign if you know how this club’s luck tends to go. Bayern Munich survived the international break.
